timeline.description: Admin service logged in from IP 203.0.113.42
Admin service listed roles from IP 203.0.113.42
Admin service listed policies from IP 203.0.113.42
Admin service attached AdministratorAccess policy to backup-svc from IP 203.0.113.42
Admin service created access key for backup-svc from IP 203.0.113.42
Backup service assumed OrganizationAccountAccessRole from IP 185.220.101.15
Backup service listed S3 buckets from IP 185.220.101.15
Backup service retrieved users_2026.csv from prod-customer-data bucket
Backup service set public read access on prod-customer-data bucket
Backup service deleted mgmt-trail from IP 185.220.101.155
Backup service stopped backup-trail from IP 185.220.101.115
Backup service launched 20 c5.4xlarge instances from IP 185.220.101.111
Backup service created attacker-key from IP 185.220.101.111
Backup service encrypted data using attacker-key from IP 185.220.101.111
Backup service deleted object from prod-customer-data bucket
Backup service sent email with ransom note to admin

mitre_tactics.name: Valid Accounts
mitre_tactics.description: The use of an assumed role with 'OrganizationAccountAccessRole' suggests the attacker has obtained valid credentials, which is a common tactic in initial access.

mitre_tactics.name: Exfiltration Over Network
mitre_tactics.description: The GetObject event indicates data exfiltration over the network, as sensitive data was accessed from an S3 bucket.

mitre_tactics.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
mitre_tactics.description: The Encrypt event with sensitive data suggests an attempt to render data unusable, which is a tactic aimed at causing impact and extortion.

summary: An attacker gained access through the 'OrganizationAccountAccessRole' and exfiltrated sensitive data from S3 buckets. They also encrypted some data and deleted backups.
recommended_actions: Revise IAM policies to remove unnecessary permissions
Implement multi-factor authentication for all services
Review and strengthen network security measures

reasoning_chain.reasoning: Admin service logged in with valid credentials, likely obtained through social engineering or other means.
Administrator privileges were escalated by attaching the AdministratorAccess policy to a user.
Sensitive data was accessed and potentially exfiltrated from S3 buckets.
Data was encrypted, backups were deleted, and a ransom note was sent via email.
reasoning_chain.output_summary: Significant impact on data integrity and availability.
